name: Darknet Diaries
author: Jack Rhysider
desc: True stories from the dark side of the internet — hacks, breaches, and cyber crime.
feedDesc: Explore true stories of the dark side of the Internet with host Jack Rhysider as he takes you on a journey through the chilling world of hacking, data breaches, and cyber crime. ¿Hablas español? Escucha en español aquí ➔ https://darknetdiaries.com/es/

episodes.title: 175: Bayrob
episodes.description: It started with a fake car listing on eBay. What looked like a simple online scam quietly grew, over more than a decade, into one of the most sophisticated cybercrime operations the FBI had ever traced. Custom malware. Opsec off the charts. Fleets of infected computers mining cryptocurrency for some

episodes.title: 174: Pacific Rim
episodes.description: For six years, Sophos fought a secret cyber war against a state-backed hacking group targeting its firewalls. This forced Sophos to drastically change tactics to properly secure their firewalls. Was it ethical? Was it effective? They disrupted nine zero-day attacks, exposed who was hacking them, and

episodes.title: 173: Tarjeteros
episodes.description: In the streets of the Dominican Republic, a new economy thrives in the shadows. It’s built not on tourism or sugar, but on stolen data. They call them tarjeteros. And they are making a lot of money from stolen credit cards. This is a story about one group of tarjeteros who came to the US, and let lo

episodes.title: 172: SuperBox
episodes.description: What if there was a device which gave you endless movies and TV shows without ads? Ok great sign me up! In this episode we interview “D3ada55”, who found such a device, but as she gazed into it, she discovered it gazing back at her. Sponsors Support for this show comes from ThreatLocker® . ThreatLoc

episodes.title: 171: Melody Fraud
episodes.description: What if the music charts you see aren’t real? What if the numbers that define success can be manufactured? We talked to Andrew, a man who has spent his career on both sides of this battle. He once profited from the loopholes in streaming platforms, but now, his job is to close them. This episode wil

episodes.title: 170: Phrack
episodes.description: Phrack is legendary. It is the oldest, and arguably the most prestigious, underground hacking magazine in the world. It started in 1985 and is still running today. In this episode we interview the Phrack staff to hear some stories about what it’s like running a hacker magazine for 40 years. phrack.o

episodes.title: 169: MoD
episodes.description: Legion of Doom, step aside. There’s a new elite hacker group in town, and they’re calling themselves Masters of Deception (MoD). With tactics that are grittier and more sophisticated than those of the LoD, MoD has targeted high-profile entities and left an indelible mark on the internet. This is par

episodes.title: 168: LoD
episodes.description: The Legion of Doom (LoD) wasn’t just a “hacker group”, it captured the essence of underground hacking in the 80s/90s. BBSes, phreaking, rival crews, and the crackdowns that changed everything. From those humble beginnings came a legacy that still echoes through modern security culture today. This is

episodes.title: 167: Threatlocker
episodes.description: A manufacturer gets hit with ransomware. A hospital too. Learn how Threatlocker stops these types of attacks. This episode is brought to you by Threatlocker. episodes.title: 166: Maxie
episodes.description: Maxie Reynolds loves an adventure, especially the kind where she’s breaking into buildings (legally). In this episode, she shares stories from her time as a professional penetration tester, including high-stakes physical intrusions, red team chaos, and the unique adrenaline of hacking the real world

episodes.title: 165: Tanya
episodes.description: Tanya Janca is a globally recognized AppSec (application security) expert and founder of We Hack Purple. In this episode, she shares wild stories from the front lines of cybersecurity. She shares stories of when she was a penetration tester to an incident responder. You can sign up for her newslette

episodes.title: 164: Oak Cliff Swipers
episodes.description: He started small, swiping cards, buying gift cards, and cashing out. It spiraled into a full‑blown criminal enterprise. episodes.description: In 2019, Ola Bini, a Swedish programmer and privacy advocate, was arrested in Ecuador for being a Russian hacker. Find Ola on X: https://x.com/olabini . episodes.title: 161: mg
episodes.description: In this episode we talk with mg ( https://x.com/ MG ), the brilliant (and notorious) hacker and hardware engineer behind the OMG Cable. A seemingly ordinary USB cable with extraordinary offensive capabilities. Learn more about mg at: o.mg.lol Sponsors Support for this show comes from ThreatLocker® .

episodes.title: 160: Greg
episodes.description: Greg Linares (AKA Laughing Mantis) joins us to tell us about how he became the youngest hacker to be arrested in Arizona. Follow Greg on Twitter: https://x.com/Laughing_Mantis . episodes.title: 159: Vastaamo

episodes.title: 19: Operation Aurora
episodes.description: In 2009, around Christmas time, something terrible was lurking in the network at Google. Google is the most popular website on the Internet. It’s so popular many people just think Google is the Internet. Google hires many of the most talented minds and has been online since the 90s. Hacking into Goo

episodes.title: 18: Jackpot
episodes.description: A man addicted to gambling finds a bug in a video poker machine that lets him win excessive amounts of money.

episodes.title: 17: Finn
episodes.description: A 14-year-old kid who finds himself bored in class decides to hack someone's twitter account and ends up with more than he bargained for.

episodes.title: 16: Eijah
episodes.description: In 2007, a hacker named Eijah got fed up with the way DRM prevented him from being able to play the content he paid for. He decided to fight back against the AACS and find a way to circumvent the DRM. By the time Eijah was done, his life wasn't the same.

episodes.title: 15: Ill Tills
episodes.description: A major retailer was hacked. Their point of sales machines were riddled with malware. Listen to hear how digital forensics and incident responders handled the situation. What malware was found? Where was it found? How was it stopped? And most importantly, how much data was leaked?

episodes.title: 14: #OpJustina
episodes.description: In 2013 a hospital was accused of conducting a medical kidnapping against a young girl name Justina. This enraged many people across the country, including members of anonymous. A DDOS attack was waged against the hospital.

episodes.title: 13: Carna Botnet
episodes.description: In 2012 the Carna Bot was built and unleashed on the world. But it didn't have any intentions on doing anything malicious. It was built just to help us all understand the Internet better. This botnet used the oldest security vulnerable in the book. And the data that came out of it was amazing.

episodes.title: 12: Crypto Wars
episodes.description: In the 1990's the Internet started to take shape. But the US goverment had strict laws regulating what type of cryptography is allowed to be used online. A few brave people stood up to the government in the name of civil rights and won the right to use strong encryption. Listen to their battle and w

episodes.title: 11: Strictly Confidential
episodes.description: What happens when an innovative tech company, that's trying to develop the next big thing, detects a hacker in their network? We hear the story from a digital forensics investigator which has a surprising result.

episodes.title: 10: Misadventures of a Nation State Actor
episodes.description: In today's world of intelligence gathering, governments hack other governments. This episode takes you on a ride with a nation state actor to see exactly how it's done.

episodes.title: 9: The Rise and Fall of Mt. Gox
episodes.description: Mt. Gox was the largest bitcoin exchange in the world. It suddenly went offline. What happened?

episodes.title: 8: Manfred (Part 2)
episodes.description: Manfred found a way to turn his passion for video games and reverse engineering into a full time business. He exploited video games and sold virtual goods and currency for real money. This was his full time job. Listen to this episode to hear exactly how he did this.

episodes.title: 7: Manfred (Part 1)
episodes.description: Manfred has had the most epic story of all online video game stories. For the last 20 years, he's been hacking online games.

episodes.title: 6: The Beirut Bank Job
episodes.description: Jayson E. Street tells us a story about the time he broke into a bank in Beirut Lebanon.

episodes.title: 5: #ASUSGATE
episodes.description: Security researcher Kyle Lovett bought a new Asus router in 2013. He found it was riddled with security vulnerabilties. He set out on a mission to resolve these vulnerabilities not only for his own router, but for thousands of others who were also vulnerable.

episodes.title: 4: Panic! at the TalkTalk Board Room
episodes.description: Mobile provider TalkTalk suffered a major breach in 2015. The CEO tried her best to keep angry customers calm and carry on. The UK government and Metropolitan Police investigate the breach. We get a rare glimpse of how the CEO handles the crisis.

episodes.title: 3: DigiNotar, You are the Weakest Link, Good Bye!
episodes.description: The 2011 DigiNotar breach changed the way browsers do security. In this episode, we learn what role a CA plays, how browsers work with CAs, and what happens when a CA is breached.

episodes.title: 2: The Peculiar Case of the VTech Hacker
episodes.description: VTech makes toy tablets, laptops, and watches for kids. In 2015, they were breached. The hacker downloaded gigs of children's data. Discover what the hacker did once he took the data.

episodes.title: 1: The Phreaky World of PBX Hacking
episodes.description: Farhan Arshad and Noor Aziz Uddin were captured 2 years after being placed on the FBI's Cyber's Most Wanted list for PBX hacking. In this episode, we explain PBX hacking and how hackers are racking up billions of dollars in phone bills. We also learn how the two men were captured.
